extraction.one_line_summary: GANNA Filippo won the final time trial despite a puncture at 1.5km to go, while BERNAL Egan secured overall victory with controlled ride.
tactical_patterns: GANNA punctured at 1.5km remaining but bike change still secured victory, showing mechanical resilience matters even with cushion.
CAVAGNA crashed 500m from finish while in virtual second, indicating flat technical finale punishes late-stage aggression.
ALMEIDA moved up overall standings significantly on final TT, revealing climbers underestimate pure time trialists' GC threat.
CARUSO gained time on BERNAL despite losing GC, showing winner rode conservatively when gap was secure.

rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: fifth consecutive Giro ITT win maintained despite late puncture, unbeaten in grand tour time trials
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: neutral: rode defensively with 1:29 margin, prioritized securing GC over time trial performance
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: lost time to second place CARUSO instead of gaining, revealing weakness in flat technical TTs
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Remi: negative: crashed in final 500m while challenging for stage podium, technical handling failure in high-pressure moment
rider_signals.ALMEIDA Joao: positive: wonderful ride moved him up in top ten, climber specialists underestimated his flat TT capability
rider_signals.CARUSO Gianni: positive: first grand tour podium at age 34, gained time on winner in final stage showing strong finishing form
